mission.title: Install and Configure Rook Ceph Operator
mission.description: Rook Ceph Operator is a cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes that automates the deployment and management of Ceph storage. It provides self-managing, self-scaling, and self-healing storage services, making it ideal for large-scale production environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of Ceph and the need for adequate resources to support its operations.

steps.title: Add Rook Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Rook Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Rook operator using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add rook-release https://charts.rook.io/release

helm repo update

```

> This adds the official Rook Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Rook Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Rook operator in the 'rook-system' namespace using the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace rook-system

helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

```

> Creates the namespace for Rook.
> Installs the Rook Ceph operator.
commands: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

steps.title: Create Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Ceph cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.3/deploy/examples/ceph/cluster.yaml

```

> Deploys the Ceph cluster configuration.

steps.title: Verify Ceph Cluster Status
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Ceph cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system get pods

kubectl -n rook-system exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

```

> Lists all pods in the rook-system namespace to verify that the Ceph components are running.
> Executes the ceph status command to check the health of the Ceph cluster.
commands: kubectl -n rook-system exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rook Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Rook operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the Ceph cluster and all associated data, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall rook-ceph --namespace rook-system

kubectl delete namespace rook-system

```

> Uninstalls the Rook Ceph operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rook Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This process includes draining the nodes if necessary, upgrading the operator, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system get pods

helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

kubectl -n rook-system get pods

```

> Check the current status of the pods before upgrading.
> Upgrades the Rook Ceph operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create ceph cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources allocated to the Ceph cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your nodes meet the minimum resource requirements (e.g., 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ceph status: HEALTH_ERR'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Ceph cluster is in an unhealthy state, possibly due to misconfiguration or resource issues.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Ceph pods for errors and ensure all nodes are healthy.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Ceph configuration is incorrect or if there are insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Review the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no available monitors'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Ceph monitors are not running or not reachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the monitor pods and ensure they are running correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Improved stability and performance of Ceph cluster management, added support for new storage classes.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl -n rook-system get pods' showing all pods in the 'Running' state, and 'ceph status' should report 'HEALTH_OK'.
codeSnippets: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3
kubectl -n rook-system exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
